title: Format Profile
description: #439 scholar-declared document LAYOUT profile (spec docs/design/2026-06-15-439-format-profile-design.md). Consumed by the Phase 7 formatter_agent (a RENDERER, 'how should this look'), NOT a verifier — kept separate from venue_profile by design (decision B). Declared-only rule (downgraded, §3): the formatter follows ONLY the supplied fields and NEVER infers a missing layout field from the venue name, institution, language/locale, or filename — the reason is consistency/reproducibility, NOT integrity, so this carries NO provenance machinery (no declared_by const, no _inferred lint). Omission of a field = NOT-DECLARED = the formatter uses its current prose default for that aspect (byte-equivalent to pre-#439 when the whole profile is absent, §5a). Venue compliance constraints take precedence over these rendering preferences (§3a). Each field is best-effort per output target (DOCX/PDF/LaTeX), §5c.

family.description: Body text font family, e.g. 'SimSun' / 'Times New Roman'. Display value only — never used to infer other fields.

size_pt.description: Body font size in points, e.g. 12 or 10.5 (五号).

body_font.description: Body text font. Omit the object or any sub-field to leave the formatter's current default for that aspect.

placement.description: Caption position relative to the figure/table. One global value in the first field set; per-object-type split (figures-below / tables-above) is a documented open item, added only on a declared need.

latin_font_family.description: Font for Latin-script tokens inside an otherwise-CJK caption, e.g. 'Fig.' set in Times New Roman (the honest model of the #436 bilingual case). Does NOT model caption ordering / numbering / separator rules — semantic caption content stays upstream.

fixed_pt.description: Fixed line spacing in points, e.g. 20. Required iff mode == fixed_pt; present with any other mode is a schema-INVALID profile (rejected, not ignored).

line_spacing.description: Line spacing. mode==fixed_pt requires fixed_pt; any other mode forbids it.

margins_cm.description: Page margins in centimeters (cm only by intent — CJK thesis use case; unit-bearing values deferred).

table_border_style.description: Table border convention. 'three_line' is the common academic three-line (booktabs-style) table.
